_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Register all test-enhancement Makefile suites as launchable dashboard cards (21 test + 9 quality categories in .iw-orch.json)

steps.description: Extend `.iw-orch.json` with 21 new test_config.categories entries (smoke + properties + properties-deep + quarantine + flake-detect + cli-contract + isolation + security-module + data-layer + route-sweep + contract-fuzz + test-assertions + e2e-smoke + e2e + perf + perf-daemon + perf-rag + perf-routes + daemon-chaos-smoke + daemon-chaos-full + visual-regression) and 9 new quality_config.categories entries (check-column-docs + security-secrets + security-sast + security-deps + diff-coverage + mutation-check + mutation-audit + dead-code + dep-check). Groups: backend / quality / e2e / perf / chaos / visual (test) and docs / security / coverage / hygiene (quality). e2e_stack=true on exactly e2e + e2e-smoke. Heavy suites (mutation-audit, daemon-chaos-full) carry wall-clock hint in description. Existing 3 test + 4 quality entries byte-identical. Verify every referenced Makefile target exists via grep; dry-run the registry-sync code path (`project_registry.py:_read_iw_orch_json`). Update `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` §8 with row 4.9 (launcher surface = DONE, link CR-00093), §11 with new top changelog entry, and bump header v1.8 → v1.9. No Python edit, no migration, no test addition.

Per-agent review of S01. CRITICAL bars: JSON parses cleanly; test_config.categories has 24 entries, quality_config.categories has 13; existing 7 entries byte-identical against main; every new entry references an existing Makefile target (regex against Makefile); e2e_stack=true scoped to exactly {e2e, e2e-smoke}; bundle=true scoped to exactly {all, all-quality}; every new entry has label+command+description+group; scope diff = exactly the 2 Impacted Paths (no dashboard/orch/tests/Makefile/migration edits); tracker §8 row 4.9 + §11 new top entry + header v1.9 all present and consistent on CR-00093 + 2026-05-28. Run `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py -v --no-cov`. Heavy-suite wall-clock hint in mutation-audit + daemon-chaos-full description fields.

Global cross-step review executing AC1–AC7 mechanically. AC1: test count = 24. AC2: quality count = 13. AC3: every new make-target exists in Makefile. AC4: e2e_stack scoped to {e2e, e2e-smoke}. AC5: existing 7 entries byte-identical against main (git show main:.iw-orch.json diff). AC6: tracker §8 row 4.9 + §11 entry + header bumped to v1.9. AC7: deferred to S11 qv-browser. Cross-step anchors: S01 report numeric anchors (test_categories_total=24, quality_categories_total=13, e2e_stack_categories=[e2e, e2e-smoke], missing_makefile_targets=[]) match live JSON. Run `make test-unit` + `uv run pytest tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py -v --no-cov`. Manifest scope.allowed_paths = exactly [.iw-orch.json, ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md].
QV: Linting (no Python touched — should pass without movement)
QV: Integration tests (includes tests/dashboard/test_route_contract_sweep.py from CR-00072 — covers the Tests + Quality launch pages)
QV: Diff coverage (CR is JSON+Markdown — no executable lines added, should pass trivially)
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks) — no secrets expected; config+tracker only

QV: Browser verification — render-only. V1: Tests page renders ≥24 cards under their groups. V2: Quality page renders ≥13 cards. V3: clicking the new `smoke` card creates a TestRun row (no wait for completion). V4: clicking the new `check-column-docs` card creates a quality-run row. V5: e2e_stack mutual exclusion (best-effort; may be n/a if nested E2E stack unavailable). V6: existing `unit` + `lint` cards still launch correctly; no console errors. Per operator choice: render + click + confirm row appears — do NOT wait end-to-end.

Self-assessment via the iw-item-analyze skill. Anchor on: (a) is the CR as small as designed (2-file diff, near-zero fix cycles); (b) did the registry abstraction deliver on 'zero Python change' or did a fix cycle leak into dashboard/; (c) was V5 (e2e_stack) exercisable or permanently n/a (suggest unit-test fallback if permanent); (d) S01 numeric anchors match S11 observed counts; (e) no sibling-project config touched; (f) S01 restated the operator's post-merge SIGHUP step; (g) heavy-suite wall-clock hints present; (h) tdd_red_evidence = 'n/a — config-only registry edit ...'.
